hours_note: More than 1 in 4 worked more than 40 hours per week in 2024.
notes: Strong upside, but deadline cycles can bleed into evenings.
High-demand analytical path with strong upside and some AI resilience because it often involves building or governing AI systems.

hours_note: Most work full time and some work more than 40 hours per week.
notes: Predictable office pattern for many roles, but a lot of task structure is AI-sensitive.

hours_note: Facilities that provide 24-hour care may require nurses to work rotating shifts and weekends and to be on call.
notes: Very durable and geographically flexible, but often costs nights, weekends, or holiday control.

hours_note: Most work full time during the school year.
notes: Calendar rhythm is predictable, but prep and grading can spill beyond the school day.

hours_note: Most work full time, and some work more than 40 hours per week. Longer hours are common at certain times of the year.
notes: Stable and structured, but repetitive knowledge work faces meaningful AI pressure.

hours_note: Most work full time and some work more than 40 hours per week.
notes: Good upside, but higher-end finance paths often pull people toward major hubs and weaker boundaries.

hours_note: Most management analysts work full time and overtime is common.
notes: Strong earnings and mobility, but travel and client demands often eat into evenings.

hours_note: Most graphic designers work full time, but schedules can vary for self-employed workers.
notes: Creative lifestyle can feel flexible, but earnings are more modest and tooling is highly AI-exposed.

hours_note: Most web developers and digital designers work full time.
notes: Combines design and tech with good upside, but interface work is being reshaped quickly by AI tooling.

hours_note: Most electricians work full time. Work schedules may include evenings and weekends and may vary during bad weather.
notes: Strong local demand and low AI risk, with a clearer end-of-day cutoff than many office jobs.

hours_note: Most flooring installers and tile and stone setters work full time.
notes: Physically demanding, but often easier to protect evenings and weekends than in elite knowledge-work tracks.

hours_note: Most construction managers work full time, and some work more than 40 hours per week.
notes: High pay with strong real-world demand, but schedules move with projects and deadlines.

hours_note: Most physicians and surgeons work full time. Some worked more than 60 hours per week in 2024.
notes: Extremely strong earnings, but the lifestyle cost is often nights, weekends, and limited schedule control.
Analytical and policy-oriented with strong boundaries for many roles, but fewer openings and more hub concentration than general business jobs.

hours_note: Most biological technicians work full time.
notes: Good science-adjacent entry path, but the earnings ceiling is lower unless you add more schooling.
